r1.prompt_summary: PM/plan-assigned (WK124-T6 verification, PM prompt 11): create tests/test_wk124_ranger_idle_soak.py (headless engine, ~6 rangers, ~10 sim-min @ DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1 + dummy SDL + fixed seed); assert over the last 3 sim-min: ranger idle-ish fraction <0.4 AND mean max distance-from-castle >15 tiles. MUST pass on the post-fix tree, would fail pre-fix. Then run the full regression sweep (qa_smoke --quick, WK67 digest, WK124 cluster, full pytest tests/). Stay in QA lane, do not edit production code, DO NOT COMMIT, DO NOT run git.
r1.what_i_changed: CREATED tests/test_wk124_ranger_idle_soak.py (1 test, green). Builds a deterministic GameEngine(headless=True): SDL_VIDEODRIVER/SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dummy + DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1 set in os.environ BEFORE config/engine import (so config.DETERMINISTIC_SIM is read True and engine.update auto-advances _sim_now_ms -> commit windows tick); set_sim_seed(20240603); engine.ai_controller = BasicAI(llm_brain=None) so idle rangers go through the no-LLM fallback into explore() (the WK124-T6 path). Spawns 6 rangers (hero_class='ranger') out of a constructed RangerGuild (mirrors tools/wk123_scenario.py guild+hero spawn pattern, ranger class), gold=0 so they aren't diverted to shopping. Neutralizes enemy/lair SPAWNING via a test-only instance monkeypatch (engine.sim.spawner.spawn / lair_system.spawn_enemies -> []) and clears engine.enemies each tick, so the soak isolates exploration (no combat pull). Runs 10 sim-min @ SIM_TICK_HZ (60Hz -> 36000 ticks); over the LAST 3 sim-min samples per-tick per-ranger 'idle-ish' (state==IDLE OR (target.type=='patrol' AND dist-to-patrol-zone <4 tiles)) and tracks each ranger's MAX distance-from-castle. Asserts idle-ish fraction <0.40 AND mean(per-ranger max dist) >15 tiles. NO production code edited; only the new test file + this log.
r1.thresholds_rationale: Thresholds chosen to meaningfully distinguish fixed-vs-broken, measured 2026-06-03 on the dev box: idle-ish fraction post-fix ~0.027 vs pre-fix (simulated by stubbing exploration._find_distant_frontier_tile->None and _roam_toward_distant_objective->False, i.e. the old empty/None behavior) ~0.80 -> 0.40 sits squarely between with wide margin. The idle-ish fraction is the LOAD-BEARING assertion (catches the late-game seize-up). mean max-dist post-fix ~76 tiles vs pre-fix ~41 (>15 either way, because the local scan still pushes rangers out in the FIRST minutes before the bubble exhausts) -> mean max-dist is the secondary corroborating proof that rangers leave the 6-10-tile near-castle bubble at all. Both thresholds documented in the test module docstring.

wk91_w2.notes: Pure-move seam + independent screenshot review + full DoD gate; no production edits, did not commit. Delegation is verified directly (this is a wrapper, not an alias -- there is no re-export to identity-check): a bare object.__new__(UrsinaRenderer) instance + a monkeypatch-spy on ursina_building_sync.sync_snapshot_buildings (patched WHERE USED, because the wrapper does a fresh lazy 'from game.graphics import ursina_building_sync') proves the wrapper calls its module fn exactly once with the renderer (self) as the first positional arg, forwards the remaining args (snapshot, world, active_ids), and returns the module fn result. The cycle break is verified two ways: statically (AST: no module-top ursina_renderer import in ursina_building_sync.py -- only TYPE_CHECKING) and dynamically (cold subprocess imports succeed in both orders). Screenshot review went beyond rubber-stamping: I ran a full-frame PIL pixel diff which pinpoints the ONLY change to a 6x9px box at the frame-time debug HUD digit (50 nonzero pixels of 1.33M) -- the entire building cluster (castle 3D mesh + houses + guild/market/inn + lair), island, units/trees, HUD and toolbar are pixel-identical, exactly matching the PM/Agent-09 note that only the fluctuating frame-time digit differs. Renderer state (_camera_active_layer/_entities/_unit_anim_state/_debug_revealed_pois/_poi_mystery_markers/_entity_render) + the _entity_in_view method correctly stayed on the renderer; both update() call sites unchanged. 877->883 suite (only additions are my 6 seam-test cases). No regressions found.

r1_kickoff.prompt_summary: Write tests/test_wk131_items_soak.py: deterministic headless 10-sim-min soak (DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1, dummy SDL) with several heroes, enemies enabled, loot POIs present. Assert (a) enemy-kill drop lands in backpack/equip, (b) hero sells backpack loot at a shop, (c) same-seed re-run is byte-identical, (d) backpack never exceeds capacity. Run full gates (pytest tests/, qa_smoke --quick, wk67 boundary). Review test_wk131_items.py / _items_ui.py coverage. Update log. DO NOT COMMIT.

soak_design.scenario: GameEngine(headless=True) + BasicAI(llm_brain=None), 4 warriors + 2 rangers homed at starting guilds, 0 starting gold, enemies/lairs/POIs FULLY enabled (WK127 lesson: no sanitized harness), 10 sim-min at SIM_TICK_HZ; run B = 5 sim-min compared against run A's 5-min checkpoint fingerprint
soak_design.subprocess_isolation: LOAD-BEARING: each soak run executes in a fresh python subprocess (test file doubles as __main__ runner emitting JSON telemetry). In-process back-to-back engine runs are NOT reproducible: module-level singletons (game/systems/navigation.py global _pathfinding_budget pending replan queue, blocked-tile caches) survive across GameEngine constructions, so a 2nd same-seed run diverges after ~2-4 sim-min (verified: in-process A/B/C all differ; two fresh processes byte-identical, finals sha1 a77f0fa915f2 @4min). Subprocesses test the production-relevant property and shield the soak from full-suite global pollution.
soak_design.measured: enemy_rolls=777 kill-loot rolls, drops_landed=43 (equipped/stored), 4 hero_sold_items sales at marketplace, 0 backpack cap violations, run B == run A checkpoint byte-identical; module runtime ~80s
soak_design.poi_substitution: engine soak does not assert a hero physically reaches a loot POI (route-dependent); POI half covered by direct seeded POIInteractionSystem/roll_poi_drop determinism + receive_item delivery test, per the kickoff's explicit fallback clause

findings_for_other_agents.finding: Cross-engine global-state residue: constructing a SECOND GameEngine in one process is not reproducible because module-level singletons persist (navigation.py _pathfinding_budget._pending_queue + blocked-tile caches at minimum). Fresh-process runs are byte-identical, so shipping determinism is fine TODAY, but an in-session 'new game'/restart reuses those singletons; any future replay/multiplayer rail needs an engine-construction reset hook. Repro: tests/test_wk131_items_soak.py module docstring (in-process A/B/C 4-min runs all diverge ~min 2-4).

Heroes only sell backpack loot as a side-effect of a shopping trip (do_shopping sells first). A hero with a full backpack but no buy-motivation keeps 'dropped' (lost) further loot. Works in practice (kill gold funds trips - 4 sales in 10 min), but consider a 'go sell when backpack full' want-predicate later.

Gap (low risk): boss-drop tests stub the event dict; nothing pins that the REAL combat-system kill event 'enemy' string matches BOSS_ENEMY_TYPES - verified manually that combat.py _normalize_enemy_type preserves snake_case ('bandit_lord'), so it works; a one-line integration assert would future-proof renames. hero_sold_items event payload untested there (now covered by the soak).

soak_design.subprocess_isolation: wk131 pattern: file doubles as __main__ runner; each scenario in a fresh python subprocess (module-singleton contamination lesson).
soak_design.scenario_main: GameEngine(headless=True) + BasicAI(llm_brain=None) (deterministic-accept decision of record), 10 heroes (6 warriors/2 rangers/2 clerics; clerics keep the party over the 0.65 approach health gate), enemies/lairs/POIs FULLY enabled, 2 constructed Herald's Posts by the castle, 4 quests armed across the 2 givers (raid_lair 140g / explore_far 140g / slay goblin x3 60g / find_poi 60g), <=15 sim-min with >=6 sim-min minimum soak; escrow debit asserted at arming; failed quests re-armed (PM decision: escrow consumed, giver re-armable).
soak_design.scenario_decline: Same engine + LLMBrain('mock'): three 40g raid offers on one giver (< the MockProvider 50g threshold -> every verdict DECLINES via the explore/retreat carrier); run monitors EVERY quest_offer commit every tick for 15.5 sim-min past the first decline.

per_type_completion.raid_lair: DRIVEN: organic first (lair hp capped 25 at arming); 60s after accept the raider is teleported adjacent, lair capped 2hp, defenders within 8 tiles capped 1hp, raider hp topped up, objective re-pinned after combat detours. Lair still dies to REAL combat -> LAIR_CLEARED -> on_lair_cleared payout.
per_type_completion.slay_enemy_type: DRIVEN: from accept+15s a 1-HP goblin spawns beside the ACCEPTING hero every 8s (cap 12); kills flow through real combat -> ENEMY_KILLED -> on_enemy_killed counter (progress 3/3).
per_type_completion.find_poi: ORGANIC in the shipped seed (accepting hero walked to the POI); position-drive fallback armed at accept+60s exercises the REAL proximity poll if a future seed needs it.
per_type_completion.explore_far: DRIVEN: position-drive to the frontier tile at accept+60s; REAL fog reveal (HERO_VISION_TILES=7 > 3-tile radius + diagonal) flips tiles SEEN and the detector poll completes.

soak_design.acceptance_is_never_forced: every accept rode the seeded 15% approach roll + walk + arrival pipeline (commits telemetry proves the walks); drives only engineer COMPLETION conditions per the T10 brief.
soak_design.measured_main: 4 quest_started (all organic), all 4 types completed, payouts exactly reward - int(reward*TAX_RATE) (45/45/105/105), 0 failures in the shipped tuning (earlier tunings: raiders died to lair defenders twice -> survival drive added), 6+ sim-min soak, 6 approach commits.
soak_design.measured_decline: 9 declines, 35 quest_offer commits, 0 accidental accepts, first decline @2.27 sim-min, watched 17.77 sim-min, ZERO commits inside any (hero,giver) 15-min decline window.
soak_design.leak_test: (d) REAL offscreen Ursina: 8 giver billboards w/ '!' marker + Herald label via the real render collaborator, killed via the REAL UrsinaRenderer._destroy_removed_entities, 3 add/remove cycles -> scene.entities returns to baseline every cycle (WK123 C1 pattern; skips cleanly without a Panda3D pipe).
soak_design.digest_backstop: (e) pins the _AI_DECISION_DIGEST golden constant in tests/test_wk67_ai_boundary.py to b73961... so a silent re-baseline cannot pass the suite.

gaps_noted: GAP (production, Agent 06/05 follow-up): a quest raider pulled into combat with lair defenders never gets re-pointed at the lair objective afterwards - the raid dangles until the hero dies or wanders off (observed twice in soak tuning; the soak documents and drives around it). No test pins 'raider resumes objective after a combat detour' because the behavior does not exist.

GAP (production, Agent 05 follow-up): destroying a Herald's Post culls its QuestGiver but an OPEN quest on that giver stays in QuestSystem.quests forever (is_target_alive only checks the quest TARGET, not the giver) - unreachable open offer + consumed escrow. No test covers post-destroyed-with-open-quest.

round_meta.wave: Wave 2 (T3): initial-goblin-wave balance harness + tuning verdict + full gate stack

round_meta.file_lane_honored: tools/wk137_balance_probe.py + tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py ONLY; config.py and game/ NOT touched

r1.prompt_summary: T3: measure the initial wave (10 goblins + 1 Goblin Warchief @ 30 sim-sec) vs the Sovereign balance bands on NORMAL. Build tools/wk137_balance_probe.py (10-seed x H(10,8,6) matrix @ shipped count + H=8 sweep count 8..14 + recommendation) and tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py (3-seed deterministic subset). Force NORMAL+assert, isolate the wave (assert 0 enemies pre-spawn), seed alternating warrior/ranger heroes, count deaths via direct refs (permadeath culling). Plus ONE realistic full-systems sanity run. Gates: digest green, full suite, qa_smoke --quick.

what_i_changed.tools/wk137_balance_probe.py: Per-cell deterministic balance harness mirroring test_wk67_ai_boundary._build_digest_engine: fresh GameEngine(headless=True) per (seed,H,count), DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1 + set_sim_seed + ai.basic_ai._AI_RNG re-seed, BasicAI(llm_brain=None) installed so heroes engage. Forces+asserts NORMAL (DEV_MODE defaults EASY). Isolates the wave (spawner.initial_no_spawn_ms=1e9 + lair_system.lairs.clear() + sim.pois=[]) and asserts enemies list EMPTY on the tick before spawn. Seeds alternating warrior/ranger heroes near castle, keeps direct refs (deaths via ref hp, not list len). Snapshots wave_event_system._active_wave_enemies after _pending_spawns drains; outcome = +120 sim-sec; win = all snapshot enemies dead AND castle in buildings & is_alive. Matrix in-process; H=8 sweep via subprocess (KINGDOM_INITIAL_WAVE_GOBLINS set before config import). Prints per-cell table + per-(H,count) summary + realistic-sanity block + verdict.
what_i_changed.tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py: 3-seed [11,23,37] x H(10,8,6) subset imported from the probe. HARD asserts (robustly true): isolation+NORMAL+composition (exactly count goblins + 1 warchief), determinism (same cell twice byte-identical), H=10 clean-win band (3/3 wins, <=1.5 deaths). H=8/H=6 difficulty bands are pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False) -- they document the measured balance gap (owned by the probe BALANCE:FAIL verdict -> PM -> Agent 05 retune) instead of permanently red-gating the whole suite; they XPASS the moment a retune makes them hold.

commands_run.result: full matrix(30) + sweep(35) + realistic sanity + verdict; BALANCE: FAIL

evidence.isolation_proof: 0 enemies alive on the tick before spawn in every isolated cell (spawner+lairs+POIs neutralized)
evidence.determinism: same (seed,H,count) twice => byte-identical outcome dict (verified seed11/H8/count10)

band_check_at_count10.H6: FAIL (10/10 wins & 0.10 deaths; want <=6 wins OR >=3.5 deaths)

evidence.sweep_conclusion: LEVER INSUFFICIENT: across counts 8..14 the H=8 mean-deaths never exceeds 0.4 -- the goblin-count lever alone CANNOT reach the H=8 [1.0,4.5] band or make H=6 hard-matched.
evidence.root_cause: Heroes (rangers especially) out-range the goblins and take ~zero damage; the isolated wave mills far from town ~45s before a quick full-HP mop-up; heroes also level up during the 30s pre-wave + cross-map travel, so the plan's balance math (lvl-1 60hp/10atk heroes meeting the wave at the town) does not hold. Matrix losses are non-clears within +120s (0-1 deaths, castle standing), not town-falls.

realistic_sanity.systems: spawner+lairs+POIs ON (no isolation), H=8, seed 11, 180 sim-sec
realistic_sanity.outcome: SANITY PASS (no exception; wave fired at 30s; warband cleared + ongoing combat); confirms schedule unshifted (Goblin Raid table wave at 110s) and that even with all systems live nobody is meaningfully threatened by the warband

evidence.digest: GREEN -- tests/test_wk67_ai_boundary.py 10 passed, AI-decision digest b73961... byte-identical (wave fires at 20s/30s, outside the 5-sim-sec digest window)
evidence.full_suite: 1860 passed, 5 skipped, 2 xfailed (the 2 xfailed are this sprint's H8/H6 balance-band markers)

r1.verdict: BALANCE: FAIL - recommend goblin_count=14 (H=10 @10: 9/10 wins / 0 deaths, H=8 @14: 5/5 wins / 0 deaths, H=6 @10: 10/10 wins / 0.1 deaths). LEVER INSUFFICIENT: the single goblin_count lever cannot reach the H=8/H=6 bands on NORMAL -- the shipped wave is far too easy at every swept count. NOT a count-bump fix.
follow_ups: PM/Sovereign decision needed: the goblin_count lever alone CANNOT satisfy the H=8 [1.0,4.5]-deaths band or the H=6 hard-match band -- a count retune (Agent 05 r2) will not help. Options to escalate: (a) stronger boss/goblin lever (warchief & goblin attack/speed up, OR 2-3 warchiefs), (b) tighten enemy spawn-to-town aggro so the wave actually pressures the town instead of milling, (c) start heroes at lvl 1 with no pre-wave level-up window, or (d) Sovereign revises the bands (current bands assume lvl-1 60hp/10atk heroes meeting the wave at the town -- not what the engine produces).
The 2 xfail markers in tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py (H8/H6 bands) should be UN-MARKED once a retune lands that makes them XPASS -- they are non-strict so they will simply flag.

Renderer/label visual proof (warchief 1.33x size, 'The Goblin Warchief' label, Bandit Lord scale, wave_incoming banner, wave-cleared) is deferred to the PM live Ursina captures per the WK standing rule -- not reproducible in a headless QA agent.

round_meta.wave: r3 QA: resolve in-process vs subprocess discrepancy (A), official canonical matrix (B), count x dist x H grid (C), harden markers (D), full gates (E)
round_meta.file_lane_honored: tools/wk137_balance_probe.py + tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py + tmp/ ONLY; config.py and game/ NOT touched

r3.prompt_summary: r3: (A) root-cause the in-process vs subprocess divergence, make ONE trustworthy path; (B) official 10-seed x H(10,8,6) matrix at shipped dist=29/count=10/jitter=2 NORMAL, confirm/refute 05; (C) grid count{10,12,14,16} x dist{27,29,31} x H(10,8,6) >=5 seeds + jitter probe, does ANY cell pass all 3 bands; (D) harden H8 xfail->hard assert (now XPASSing), keep H6 xfail with measured-ceiling comment, suite green-or-xfail; (E) probe + wk137 pytest + digest + full suite + qa_smoke gates.

part_A_root_cause.finding: Wave geometry (get_rng('wave_events') bearing in _near_anchor_tile) AND worldgen are seeded from determinism._BASE_SEED at ENGINE-CONSTRUCTION time. GameEngine/SimEngine.__init__ internally calls set_sim_seed(config.SIM_SEED). In the IN-PROCESS path config.SIM_SEED defaults to 1 (env SIM_SEED unset), so engine construction RESETS _BASE_SEED back to 1 every cell -> every in-process 'seed' shared ONE wave bearing (uniform=0.7139... constant; lairs placed identically). Only the AI RNG (_AI_RNG/_ai_rng, re-seeded in run_cell) varied. So 05's 'official 10-seed in-process matrix' was effectively 10 hero-AI variations on a SINGLE wave layout (biased). SUBPROCESS sets SIM_SEED env per cell BEFORE config import -> config.SIM_SEED==seed -> engine keeps _BASE_SEED==seed -> worldgen + wave bearing genuinely vary per seed.
part_A_root_cause.evidence: Empirically: in-process wave rng constant 0.7139620318355747 across seeds 11/23/97; subprocess 0.494/0.443/0.749. In-process _BASE_SEED==1 after every build regardless of requested seed; subprocess ==seed. In-process H8 deaths [0,2,3] seeds 11,23,37 vs subprocess [1,1,0]. Castle pos fixed at map centre (not the differentiator); the differentiator is wave bearing + worldgen terrain.
part_A_root_cause.canonical_path: SUBPROCESS (run_cell_subprocess) is CANONICAL for ALL balance numbers (matrix/sweep/grid). run_cell (in-process) RETAINED only for the fast pytest harness-contract guardrails, and FIXED to re-apply set_sim_seed(seed) + re-seed wave_event_system.rng AFTER engine build so its bearing matches the subprocess exactly (verified identical 0.494/0.443/0.749); worldgen terrain still differs in-process and cannot be retrofitted (documented). Probe main()/run_matrix/run_sweep/run_grid all route through the subprocess pool.
part_A_root_cause.implication: 05's r2/r3 dist+count sweeps were measured in-process (single wave geometry). QUALITATIVE conclusions hold under the canonical path; exact per-cell numbers shifted (05 H10 9/10 wins/1.00 -> canonical 10/10/1.00; 05 H8 1.30 -> canonical 1.10; H6 0.90 == 0.90).

part_B_official_canonical_matrix.config: dist=29, count=10, jitter=2, NORMAL forced+asserted, isolation proven (0 enemies pre-spawn), 10 seeds [11,23,37,41,53,67,71,83,97,101], CANONICAL subprocess path
part_B_official_canonical_matrix.H10: 10/10 wins, mean_deaths 1.00 (max 3), clear 27.1s -> PASS (>=9 wins AND <=1.5)
part_B_official_canonical_matrix.H8: 10/10 wins, mean_deaths 1.10 (max 3), clear 25.5s -> PASS (>=7 wins AND in [1.0,4.5])
part_B_official_canonical_matrix.H6: 10/10 wins, mean_deaths 0.90 (max 2), clear 31.6s -> FAIL (need <=6 wins OR >=3.5 deaths)
part_B_official_canonical_matrix.vs_05: CONFIRMS 05's band conclusions (H10 PASS, H8 PASS, H6 FAIL). Numbers slightly differ (05 in-process = 1 wave geometry): 05 H10 9/10 wins/1.00 vs canonical 10/10/1.00; 05 H8 1.30 vs canonical 1.10; H6 0.90==0.90. Verdict identical, evidence base now trustworthy (10 independent wave geometries).

part_C_grid.method: count{10,12,14,16} x dist{27,29,31} x H(10,8,6), 5-seed canonical screen, jitter=2; proportional bands (H10 win%>=0.9 & deaths<=1.5; H8 win%>=0.7 & deaths in[1.0,4.5]; H6 win%<=0.6 OR deaths>=3.5)
part_C_grid.all_three_bands_cell: NO - none. H6 is False at every (dist,count). H6 stays 100% wins everywhere; H6 mean-deaths CEILING across the whole grid = 1.8 (best cell dist=31/count=16).
best_3_cells_three_band_readout: dist=31 count=16: H10 PASS(1.2) | H8 PASS(2.2) | H6 FAIL(1.8 deaths, 5/5 wins) - highest H6 in grid, still half the 3.5 floor
dist=29 count=16: H10 PASS(1.2) | H8 PASS(2.4) | H6 FAIL(0.6)
dist=27 count=16: H10 PASS(1.0) | H8 PASS(1.8) | H6 FAIL(1.2)
part_C_grid.ten_seed_h6_ceiling_confirm: dist=31/c16: 10/10 wins, 1.8 deaths (max 3); dist=27/c16: 10/10, 1.2; dist=31/c14: 10/10, 1.1 -> ceiling ~1.8 over 10 seeds.

jitter_probe_dist31_count16_5seed.conclusion: jitter 0/1/3/4 do NOT unlock H6 (best 2.0 at j4) and j4 pushes H10 to max-5 deaths (near-loss). Dispersion does not separate the bands.

part_C_grid.structural_finding: CONFIRMS 05: hero deaths are dispersion-driven and scale TOGETHER across hero counts. 6 clumped heroes near the castle are the SAFEST config (100% wins at every setting). Making the wave lethal to 6 heroes over-kills the 10-line first. H6 band unreachable with dist/count/jitter; needs a Sovereign lever (pre-wave fog-reveal leveling cap / +1 warchief on NORMAL / band revision).

part_D_markers.shipped_config_unchanged: count=10/dist=29/jitter=2 (Part C found NO all-three cell, so no retune proposed; QA does not change config regardless of lane).
part_D_markers.H8: CONVERTED xfail(strict=False) -> HARD ASSERT. Now uses canonical subprocess 5-seed subset (5/5 wins, 1.00 mean deaths). Lower band edge but DETERMINISTIC (never flaky); a future change that makes the wave too easy again will correctly red-gate.
part_D_markers.H10: unchanged hard assert (in-process subset, 3/3 wins, 1.33 mean <=1.5).
part_D_markers.H6: KEPT xfail(strict=False) with an expanded comment quoting the MEASURED CEILING (~1.8 across the whole Part-C grid; shipped config 0.90) + root cause + Sovereign levers. Documents the gap without red-gating.
part_D_markers.band_tests_now_canonical: H8/H6 band fixtures use run_cell_subprocess so the suite's band claims match the official matrix; harness-contract guardrails stay on fast in-process run_cell.

commands_run.result: canonical matrix(30) + Part C grid(180) + sweep(35) + realistic sanity + verdict; RECOMMENDATION 10, 'BALANCE: 2/3 bands PASS ... H6 STRUCTURALLY UNREACHABLE (ceiling 1.8) ... Ship as-is with H6 documented'. ALL-THREE-BANDS CELL: NO.
4 passed, 1 xfailed in 93.24s (H8 now hard-passes; H6 xfail)
1864 passed, 5 skipped, 1 xfailed in 717.22s (0 failed, 0 unexpected; r1 was 1860 passed/2 xfailed; delta = H8 xfail->pass)
DONE: PASS (determinism guard + base/intent_bounty/hero_stuck/no-enemies/mock-LLM/quest_panel/conversation/direct_prompt profiles all exit_code=0)

evidence.part_A_proof: in-process wave rng constant 0.7139620318355747 across seeds 11/23/97; subprocess 0.494/0.443/0.749. Re-seed fix makes in-process bearing match subprocess exactly.
evidence.digest_status: byte-identical (b73961) - only tools/+tests/ touched, game/ & config.py untouched.
evidence.suite_delta: r1 1860 passed/5 skipped/2 xfailed -> r3 1864 passed/5 skipped/1 xfailed (H8 marker converted to a passing hard assert).

r3.recommendation_to_pm: SHIP AS-IS at the shipped config (count=10/dist=29/jitter=2). 2/3 bands PASS on the trustworthy canonical 10-seed matrix (H10 10/10 wins 1.00 deaths; H8 10/10 wins 1.10 deaths). H6 is DOCUMENTED as structurally unreachable (grid ceiling 1.8 deaths vs 3.5 floor; 100% H6 wins everywhere; jitter does not help). NO one-line retune exists - Part C found NO all-three-bands cell. Closing H6 needs a Sovereign lever (pre-wave fog-reveal leveling cap / +1 warchief on NORMAL / H6 band revision), owned by 05/06/Sovereign, out of QA lane. Suite is green-or-xfail (0 failed, 0 unexpected).
follow_ups: PM/Sovereign decision: accept the documented H6 gap, OR authorize 05/06 to add a Sovereign lever (pre-wave leveling cap is cleanest - attacks the root cause) and re-run this probe.
If a leveling-cap lever lands, the H6 xfail in tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py will XPASS - un-mark it to a hard assert at that point.

@'
import json
from pathlib import Path
paths = [
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_05_GameplaySystemsDesigner.json'),
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_06_AIBehaviorDirector_LLM.json'),
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_08_UX_UI_Director.json'),
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_09_ArtDirector_Pixel_Animation_VFX.json'),
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_10_PerformanceStability_Lead.json'),
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_12_ToolsDevEx_Lead.json'),
    Path('.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_14_SoundDirector_Audio.json'),
]
for path in paths:
    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    sprint = data['sprints'].get('wk143_dragon_hunt_showcase')
    if sprint is None:
        raise SystemExit(f'{path}: missing sprint entry')
    print(f'{path.name}: OK rounds={list(sprint.get("rounds", {}).keys())}')
'@ | python -

screenshots_wk52_pin_alerts.visual_verdict: Headless scenario frames watch card (expanded shows HP/level/name Gareth + live radar), minimized variant, radar with colored entity dots and pinned hero emphasis, and memorial overlay (In Memoriam + class/stats/epitaph). Interactive items in pm_human_retest_request (toast timing, Recall flash, 30s cooldown) require Jaimie live play.

pm_human_retest_request: 1. Pin a hero. Confirm watch card appears above minimap.
2. Deselect. Confirm left menu height adjusts.
3. Watch card minimized = 14px header peeks above minimap. Click to expand.
4. Radar shows colored dots: gold=heroes, red=enemies, blue-grey=buildings, dark-red=lairs, cyan=guild.
5. Pinned hero dot has white outline on radar.
6. HP drops below 25%: HUD toast + Recall button flashes red 3x.
7. Wait 30s -- low-health alert must NOT re-fire until HP drops again.
8. Hero levels up: toast '{name} reached level {N}!'.
9. Hero enters inn: toast '{name} is resting at the inn.'.
10. Bounty claimed: toast '{name} collected a bounty!'.
11. Click Memorial button. Full-screen pause overlay opens.
12. Heroes stop moving while memorial open.
13. Overlay shows name, class, level, stats, epitaph.
follow_ups: Jaimie: run pm_human_retest_request checklist live (pygame recommended: python main.py --renderer pygame --provider mock).
